titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Phelps to Michael Cahill, 31 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Phelps

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the librarian of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI), Robert Phelps, to solicitor Michael Frederick Cahill regarding the change of name of Dr Aimai Maneckji Cooper. According to Phelps Cooper will need to send on a declaration of identity, a postal sum and her college diplomas if possible.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
31st MARCH 16. M.F. Cahill, Esq.,
Solicitor,
Ilford, Essex. Dear Sir, I beg to acknowledge the receipt of your letter
of the 28th inst., asking the College to change the
name of Miss Aimai G.M. Cooper on the College roll.
Before this change can be registered it will be
necessary for Miss Cooper to forward a Statutory
Declaration of identity, a Postal order for the sum of
one guinea and, if possible, the College diplomas for
indorsement. As the College will meet on next Friday
the 7th prox., possibly Miss Cooper will forward theses
papers early in the week so as to get the matter through
at once. Yours faithfully,
Librarian, R.C.P.I.
